Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal odor, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, mild ketonuria) further support renal and possible hepatic involvement. HbA1c is low, indicating no chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation and assessment for possible acute or chronic kidney disease and further hepatic workup.
